name: Google Images Search
description: Search Google Images and extract image titles + alt text + thumbnail URIs from the udm=2 results grid.

browser_automation.description: Open Google Images results (modern udm=2 grid). Returns title (h3) + alt text + the (usually data-URI) thumbnail src. Full-resolution image URLs are NOT directly available without clicking each result.

sequences.name: google_images_search
sequences.description: Open the Google Images results grid and return what's visible without per-image clicks.

required_variables.name: search_query
required_variables.description: Image search query. Aliases: query, q.

steps.name: Wait for at least one image card

help.summary: Search Google Images and return title/alt/thumbnail for each card. Full image URLs require per-card clicks (not done here).

parameters.name: search_query
parameters.description: Image search query. Aliases query and q work too.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google images --param search_query="golden retriever puppy"
rzn-browser run google images --param query="northern lights aurora"

help.returns: Array of { alt_text, thumbnail_src }. alt_text doubles as the image caption/title; thumbnail_src is usually a base64 data: URI (often a placeholder transparent GIF for above-the-fold lazy-loaded slots), not the original image URL.
notes: Google Images now serves under udm=2 (the old tbm=isch redirects there) and hides full image URLs behind a click-to-open side panel. This workflow returns only what's visible in the grid without clicking.
If you need the original image URL for each result, build a follow-up workflow that clicks each card and reads the side-panel a[href] — that's a separate (slow, brittle) effort.
The grid is virtualized; only viewport-visible images get extracted.
